full_response:
The Schwarzschild line–element  

ds² = −(1 − 2GM/r) dt² + dr²/(1 − 2GM/r) + r²(dθ² + sin²θ dφ²)  

is written in the standard coordinates x^a = (t,r,θ,φ).

The world-line specified in the problem is  

x^a(λ) = ( t(λ), r(λ)=r₀, θ(λ)=θ₀, φ(λ)=φ₀ ) ,  r₀ > 2GM .

1.  Construct the tangent (4-velocity) vector  

T^a ≡ dx^a/dλ  
  = ( dt/dλ , 0 , 0 , 0 ).

2.  If we choose the parameter λ to be the proper time τ of the particle that sits at fixed (r₀,θ₀,φ₀), the normalization condition g_{ab}U^aU^b = −1 gives  

g_{tt}(U^t)² = −1 ⇒ −(1−2GM/r₀)(U^t)² = −1  
 ⇒ U^t = dt/dτ = 1/√(1−2GM/r₀).

Hence the properly normalized 4-velocity (tangent) is  

U^a = ( (1−2GM/r₀)^{−1/2} , 0 , 0 , 0 ) .

Either expression (unnormalized or normalized) is a valid tangent; the second is the one most often used because it has unit timelike norm.

\boxed{ \displaystyle T^a = \left( \frac{dt}{d\lambda},\, 0,\, 0,\, 0 \right)
\;\; ,\qquad
U^a = \left( \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-2GM/r_0}},\, 0,\, 0,\, 0 \right) \text{ for } \lambda = \tau }

Confidence: 0.31
